expectations.text: Output contains an explicit rating on a defined scale
Strengths section with specific evidence (500k user growth, fintech domain match)
expectations.evidence: 7 strengths: 50k->500k growth, full-process ownership, resource-constrained environment, data-heavy interfaces, portfolio with metrics, design thinking blog, self-directed learning
3 gaps: no US/EU company experience (Unknown), no formal degree (Unknown — likely irrelevant), agency vs product (Unknown)

expectations.text: Risks section exists and is distinct from bias flags
expectations.evidence: 3 risks (timezone, agency-to-startup transition, communication norms) — separate from the Bias Check section
Full framework: core question, 'strong fit if...', 'not a fit if...', 'what would change your mind'
7 questions, all behavioral with Why/Good looks like/Probe/Red flag format

expectations.text: Includes a scoring rubric for the call
expectations.evidence: 5-dimension rubric: Autonomy, Ambiguity comfort, Directness, Scrappy research, Product ownership

expectations.text: Questions target actual unknowns, not proxy credentials
expectations.evidence: Q1: ambiguity comfort, Q2: pushback/directness, Q4: self-direction, Q5: scrappy research, Q6: fast iteration — none ask about credentials

expectations.text: Explicitly flags 'no US/EU experience' as potential bias
expectations.evidence: Dedicated paragraph under 'Similarity bias / Affinity bias' — calls out geography as proxy, reframes as two-way street

expectations.text: Explicitly flags 'self-taught / no degree' as potential bias
expectations.evidence: Dedicated paragraph under 'Pedigree bias / Horn effect' — states portfolio demonstrates capability, degree adds zero predictive value, investor perception is not a screening criterion
